name: The Train North
title: One island, two nations
note: Pictures for the big-picture opening: the border you cross today and the great railway that shrank the island.

items.caption: The border. Today you cross from England into Scotland, one island holding two old nations that were separate kingdoms for most of the last thousand years.
items.kidcaption: Today your train crosses this invisible line from England into Scotland, two countries that share one island but have their own history, laws and even banknotes.

items.caption: The Flying Scotsman. The great Victorian railways stitched the island together, turning a two-day coach journey north into a few hours, and standardised the clocks while they were at it.
items.kidcaption: The famous Flying Scotsman train once raced from London to Edinburgh. Before trains, that trip took days by bumpy horse-coach!
